design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore non-linear stages, collect treasures and map pieces, defeat bosses, return to stages with new cane upgrades to access hidden areas
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Collecting optional map pieces to unlock secret final stage versus completing game through five main stages; resource management of collected money for cane upgrades and recovery items
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Scrooge McDuck as globe-trotting treasure hunter, discovering hidden riches across exotic locations (Niagara Falls, Egypt, Scotland, Mu, Bermuda Triangle)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to player choice and non-linearity with stage selection freedom; rewards thorough exploration and revisitation through optional content discovery; narrative integrates treasure collection as both mechanical and thematic goal; optional sixth stage accessible only through 100% map collection creates 'true ending' incentive structure

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives purchasable with collected money from Scrooge's store
difficulty.checkpointing: Stages can be revisited after completion; money and items persist across sessions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls progression pace through stage selection order; can grind for money to purchase recovery items and extra lives; cane upgrades directly facilitate access to previously unreachable areas

progression.upgrade_system: Cane upgrades obtainable from Gyro Gearloose; upgrades grant access to new and hidden areas in each level
progression.unlock_mechanism: Map piece collection (5 pieces required for six optional stages); cane upgrades unlock gated areas within stages
replayability_hooks: Revisit completed levels to collect additional money and items
Hunt for hidden map pieces in treasure chests throughout stages
Discover new paths with upgraded cane tools
Optional sixth stage accessible only through complete map collection
Multiple endings based on money status at final confrontation

world_building_techniques: Stage-based exotic locations with distinct visual themes
Boss characters tied to location flavor and treasure types
Narrative framing: Huey presents torn map piece to Scrooge, inspiring expedition
Character arcs: Webby kidnapping and rescue drives third act
Thematic ending: Treasure loss versus family safety resolution with ending variation based on player resource management

boss_design.philosophy: Each of five main stages features unique boss character guarding stage treasure; final confrontation reveals true antagonist (D-1000 robot masquerading as Flintheart Glomgold)
notable_bosses: Stage bosses at Niagara Falls, Pirate Ship (Bermuda Triangle), Mu, Egypt, Scotland
D-1000 (shapeshifting robot final boss disguised as Flintheart Glomgold)

faction_indicators: Scrooge McDuck (protagonist adventurer seeking paternal legacy)
McDuck family (Huey, Webby, Launchpad) supporting Scrooge's expedition
danger_signals: Webby kidnapped as ransom by antagonist
D-1000 robot assassination attempt disguised as negotiation
Glomgold's final sabotage: sinking pirate ship to destroy treasures and protagonists
environmental_storytelling: Five geographically distinct stages establish world scope and adventure tone
Treasure types reflect regional characteristics and Scrooge's collecting obsession
Hidden map pieces encourage environmental exploration and discovery
Cane interaction mechanics (pulling levers, firing cannons, using springy flowers) create stage-specific puzzle language
